{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Winawer Defense| |rd| |bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|| |pd|pd|= | | |nd| | | | | |= | | | | |pd|pd| | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |nl| | |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= || }} =Winawer Defense= ===3...f5=== The Winawer Defense, also known as the Gothic Defense, is a surprising response by Black instead of transposing into a Four Knight's Game. Black gambits the pawn on f5; if white accepts, the gambit creates an equalizing position for black and black often plays 4...Nf6. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}
'''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. Nc3 f5'''

fxe4		 5

Nf6		 +/=
